summary: Fighter-focused decomp PR split from #2345. It replaces placeholder comments with implementations in three fighter files: common catch-pull animation logic, Kirby hat matrix/Pichu-hat setup logic, and common fighter dead-up/rebirth/accessory-position logic. The PR body claims six 100% matches, while the bot report confirms five new 100% matches and lists ftCo_800D41C4 as a 98.33% improvement rather than a full match.

key_files.role: Implements ftCo_CatchPull_Anim for Link/Young Link/Samus hookshot or grapple pull transition checks.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Matches table and function description
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists ftCo_CatchPull_Anim as 100.00%

key_files.role: Adds baselib/jobj include and implements ftKb_UnkMtxFunc0 plus ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4 for Kirby copied-hat matrix/display and Pichu hat initialization.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4 implementation with pragma dont_inline
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists both Kirby functions as 100.00%

key_files.role: Implements ftCo_800D41C4, ftCo_Rebirth_Phys, and fn_800D54A4 in common fighter state/physics/accessory code.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists ftCo_Rebirth_Phys and fn_800D54A4 as 100.00%, ftCo_800D41C4 as 98.33%

what_changed.text: Added ftCo_CatchPull_Anim, using Fighter kind dispatch for Link/Young Link and Samus item pointers, checking hookshot/grapple item vars, and calling fn_800DA1D8 when the pull animation should transition.
Added ftKb_UnkMtxFunc0, which early-returns when Kirby has no hat jobj or a flag is unset, copies a head-bone matrix to the hat jobj, sets JOBJ matrix flags, marks it dirty, and dispatches display.

Added ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4 under a push/pop dont_inline pragma block, allocating two hat data objects and wiring Pichu hat descriptor, visibility lookup, animation, color overlay, and model scale update.
Added ftCo_800D41C4, which sets dead-up related flags/counters, changes motion state to ftCo_MS_DeadUpStarIce, plays death SFX, notifies player/common systems, and marks x68.

Added ftCo_Rebirth_Phys, computing respawn platform target positions from stage/player positions when applicable and deriving self velocity, or copying velocity from another player entity when already-respawned.
Added fn_800D54A4, which synchronizes Y position against a partner entity when needed and sets an accessory jobj translation from current position and facing direction.

smart_moves.text: The PR was narrowed to fighter-related matches split from #2345, keeping the slice focused on ft files and claimed matched functions.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Split from #2345 — fighter-related 100% matches only

smart_moves.text: The Kirby matrix callback uses existing baselib JObj helpers and flags rather than open-coded matrix/assert behavior, adding the needed jobj header include.
The catch-pull function uses typed fighter/item union arms for Link, Young Link, and Samus item state rather than raw pointer offsets for those accesses.

decomp_lessons.text: For fighter animation callbacks that branch by character kind, a switch over fp->kind can preserve readable character-specific typed accesses while still matching; here Link/Young Link share the link hookshot path and Samus uses the samus grapple path.

For JObj matrix callbacks, existing HSD_JObj helpers and flag constants can be the source-level form: get bone matrix, copy it to the target jobj, set JOBJ_USER_DEF_MTX/JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_PARENT/JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT, mark dirty, then dispatch display.

A PR title/body claim of 100% should be cross-checked against bot output; this slice shows the body claims ftCo_800D41C4 as 100%, but the bot reports it as 98.33%, so intake should preserve the discrepancy rather than record it as a verified full match.

naming_conventions.text: Common fighter local variables use short role names where clear: fp for Fighter*, ip for Item*, gobj for Fighter_GObj*/HSD_GObj*, jobj for HSD_JObj*, and bone_mtx for a matrix pointer.
Unrecovered or weakly understood symbols remain address/field-style, such as fn_800D54A4, ftCo_800D41C4, x2227_b6, x221F_b4, x610_color_rgba, and new_var/temp locals.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: PAD_STACK was used narrowly in multiple newly implemented functions: 16 bytes in ftCo_CatchPull_Anim, 8 bytes in ftKb_UnkMtxFunc0, 8 bytes in ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4, 12 bytes in ftCo_Rebirth_Phys, and 8 bytes in fn_800D54A4.

ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4 is wrapped in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop; the PR body says this preserves asm layout, but the slice does not include adjacent-function objdiff details beyond the bot match report.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Wrapped in #pragma dont_inline to preserve the asm layout

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Some expressions use casts or stack-shaping locals likely for matching, including u8 sp14[0x88], u8 _[8], duplicated assignment Fighter* fp = fp = gobj->user_data, and pointer arithmetic into new_var->hat_dynamics[4].
Typed field/union access was used for known fighter and item state instead of adding new raw offsets in the catch-pull implementation.

review_feedback.text: No inline review comments were present in the loaded slice. A review by jurrejelle approved the PR with an empty body.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]

notes: Loaded diff and changed-file metadata are complete for the slice and directly show the code changes.

The PR body's six 100% match claim conflicts with the bot report, which confirms five new matches and lists ftCo_800D41C4 as 98.33%; this intake treats ftCo_800D41C4 as not independently verified 100% by the bot evidence.

The PR body includes a section titled 'What Claude thinks these functions do'; those semantic descriptions are useful context but should be treated as author-provided interpretation, not definitive reverse-engineering proof.
No inline review comments are available, so reviewer rationale or requested changes cannot be inferred.
The slice includes bot summary but not full objdiff/build logs or adjacent-function regression details.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Cross-check PR match claims against bot report
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2402, the body lists six functions as 100%, but the bot report confirms five 100% new matches and records ftCo_800D41C4 as 98.33%. Curated records should preserve verification discrepancies instead of flattening them into the PR title/body claim.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use typed fighter and item union arms for character-specific callback state
accepted_candidate_records.text: ftCo_CatchPull_Anim matched using fp->fv.lk.xC / fp->fv.ss.x223C and item var union arms for linkhookshot and samusgrapple inside a fighter-kind switch.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Kirby hat JObj matrix callbacks can use baselib JObj helpers directly
accepted_candidate_records.text: ftKb_UnkMtxFunc0 matched while using HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr, HSD_JObjCopyMtx, JObj flag constants, HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty, and HSD_JObjDispAll, with <baselib/jobj.h> included.

source_update_candidates.title: Document that PR body match tables can disagree with bot verification
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a curation/intake note that claimed 100% matches in a PR title/body should be cross-checked against bot or objdiff output. PR #2402 claims ftCo_800D41C4 as 100%, while the bot report lists it as 98.33%. This is an intake/verification hygiene point, not a decomp coding standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could improve knowledge-curation accuracy by preventing overstatement of match status from PR prose alone.

source_update_candidates.title: Fighter Kirby init file contains Kirby copied-hat setup and matrix callback work
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c is evidence for Kirby copied-fighter hat matrix/display callbacks and Pichu hat initialization, including ftKb_UnkMtxFunc0 and ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible path-scope search aid; curator should verify against current source before adding as a path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not record ftCo_800D41C4 as a verified 100% match from this slice; bot evidence says 98.33% despite the PR body table.
Do not infer reviewer reasoning from the approval; the review body is empty and there are no inline review comments.

Do not promote the use of #pragma dont_inline or PAD_STACK as a general accepted style from this PR alone; the slice shows usage and a bot match for ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4, but not adjacent regression proof or broader justification.
Do not treat the author-provided 'What Claude thinks' descriptions as definitive symbol naming authority.
